plate2N 0.0.9

Get concentration of standard curve from metadata

Source: R/standard_curve.R
extract_curve.Rd

Get concentration of standard curve from metadata

Usage

extract_curve(metadata, pipetting_direction = "top_down")

Arguments

metadata

A tibble following a similar structure as metadata, see documentation of `metadata for more details

pipetting_direction

Can only be "top_down" (default) or "bottom_up". A top_down pipetting means that the curve was pipetted vertically (in a single column of the 96-well plate), with the smallest value (blank) in row A and the highest value in row H. Conversely, bottom_up pipetting would have the blank in row H and the most concentrated solution in row A

Value

A tibble with 3 columns: plate_id, row (corresponding to plate-row, from A to H) and std_conc containing the concentrations as given in the std_conc column of metadata.

See also

metadata

Examples

metadata
#> # A tibble: 5 × 17
#>   dataset plate_id date  time  sampling_time std_column std_sp std_unit std_prep
#>   <chr>   <chr>    <lgl> <lgl> <chr>         <chr>      <chr>  <chr>    <chr>   
#> 1 Nmin    NO3_1F1  NA    NA    t1            1-12       NO3    mg NO3-… H2O     
#> 2 Nmin    NO3_1F2  NA    NA    t1            1-12       NO3    mg NO3-… H2O     
#> 3 Nmin    NO3_1F3  NA    NA    t1            1-12       NO3    mg NO3-… H2O     
#> 4 Nmin    NO3_1F4  NA    NA    t1            1-12       NO3    mg NO3-… H2O     
#> 5 Nmin    NO3_1F5  NA    NA    t1            1-12       NO3    mg NO3-… H2O     
#> # ℹ 8 more variables: std_conc <chr>, sample_dilution <chr>,
#> #   extractant_column <lgl>, extractant_sp <chr>, extractant_unit <chr>,
#> #   extractant_conc <dbl>, empty_column <lgl>, wait_min <chr>
extract_curve(metadata)
#> # A tibble: 40 × 4
#>    dataset plate_id row   std_conc
#>    <chr>   <chr>    <chr>    <dbl>
#>  1 Nmin    NO3_1F1  A          0  
#>  2 Nmin    NO3_1F1  B          0.5
#>  3 Nmin    NO3_1F1  C          1  
#>  4 Nmin    NO3_1F1  D          2  
#>  5 Nmin    NO3_1F1  E          4  
#>  6 Nmin    NO3_1F1  F          8  
#>  7 Nmin    NO3_1F1  G         16  
#>  8 Nmin    NO3_1F1  H         24  
#>  9 Nmin    NO3_1F2  A          0  
#> 10 Nmin    NO3_1F2  B          0.5
#> # ℹ 30 more rows
